home.title: Kiro Account Manager
home.subtitle: Manage your Kiro IDE accounts, smart switching, quota monitoring
settings.title: Kiro Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure Kiro IDE models, proxy, and other settings. Changes take effect immediately.
settings.modelDesc: Select the default AI model and optionally lock it to prevent changes
settings.lockModelDesc: When enabled, automatically monitors and restores the model setting in Kiro configuration to prevent changes by other operations
settings.accountDesc: Configure features like automatic account refresh
settings.autoRefreshDesc: Periodically refresh expired or soon-to-expire account tokens to keep accounts valid
settings.autoChangeMachineIdDesc: Automatically reset Kiro IDE's machine ID when switching accounts to avoid multi-account association
settings.browserDesc: Configure the browser used for OAuth login (defaults to incognito mode)
settings.browserPlaceholder: Leave empty to use system default browser
settings.proxyDesc: Configure HTTP proxy for Kiro IDE (syncs with http.proxy in settings.json)
settings.kiroInfoDesc: Device and session information read from local Kiro IDE
settings.browserTip: Leave empty to use system default browser. Click 'Detect' to automatically find installed browsers
settings.proxyTip: Leave empty to not use proxy. Click 'Detect' to automatically get system proxy settings. Click 'Apply' after changes to take effect
settings.redeemServerDesc: Configure LicenseSystem service address for card redemption feature
settings.noBrowserFound: No browser detected in common paths, please enter path manually
settings.noProxyConfigured: System not configured with proxy, please enter manually
settings.proxyNotEnabled: System proxy configured but not enabled
settings.tunModeHint: No proxy configuration needed in TUN mode, network traffic is automatically forwarded
settings.resetRequiresClose: Resetting device ID requires closing Kiro IDE first
settings.bindMachineIdDesc: Each account uses a fixed machine ID, automatically restores corresponding machine ID when switching
settings.useBoundMachineIdDesc: When enabled, uses account-bound machine ID; when disabled, generates new random machine ID each time
settings.kiroRunningNeedClose:
Kiro IDE is detected running, need to close it before resetting machine ID.

Close Kiro IDE and continue?
settings.confirmResetMachineId: Are you sure you want to reset all machine IDs?
settings.machineIdResetSuccess:
Machine ID reset!

Start Kiro IDE now?
settings.confirmRestoreMachineGuid: Are you sure you want to restore system machine GUID to backup value?
settings.requiresAdmin: This operation requires administrator privileges, may need to run program as administrator.
settings.confirmResetSystemMachineGuid:
Are you sure you want to generate a new system machine GUID?

This operation will:
• Generate a new random UUID
• Write to system registry
• May affect some software's authorization verification
• Requires administrator privileges
• Recommend backing up current value first
settings.machineGuidDescMac: Manage macOS system machine code (implemented by overwriting files, does not affect hardware UUID)
settings.adminWarning1: Modifying system machine code requires administrator privileges
settings.adminWarning2: May affect some software's authorization verification
settings.adminWarning3: Recommend backing up current value before modification
settings.appDataDirDesc: View and manage application data storage location
settings.appDataDirTip: This directory contains account data, configuration files, logs, and other application data
settings.kiroIdePathDesc: Specify Kiro IDE installation location for account switching feature
settings.kiroPathTip: Leave empty to use system default installation path. Click 'Browse' to select custom installation location
settings.macOSNoteDesc: macOS implements machine code modification by writing to Kiro IDE's machineid file, no administrator privileges required.
settings.enableCodebaseIndexingDesc: When enabled, Kiro automatically indexes codebase for better code understanding and search functionality
settings.trustedCommandsDesc: Configure trust level when Kiro executes Shell commands (requires IDE restart to take effect)
settings.trustedCommandsAllConfirmMessag: When enabled, Kiro can automatically execute any Shell commands, including installation scripts, file modifications, and Git destructive operations. This significantly expands risk surface, continue?
settings.trustedCommandsWildcardBlocked: common mode does not allow *, switch to 'All Trust' if you need to trust all
settings.agentSettingsDesc: Configure Kiro Agent's autonomous mode, feature toggles, and notification options
settings.trustedToolsDesc: List of tools Agent can auto-use, comma-separated (e.g., webFetch, remote_web_search)
settings.configureMCPEnabled: Enabled - Allow Powers to register MCP services
settings.configureMCPDisabled: Disabled - Do not auto-register MCP services
settings.telemetryDesc: Control data types collected by Kiro IDE
settings.closeWindowBehaviorDesc: Set behavior when close button is clicked
settings.minimizeToTrayEnabled: ✓ When closing window, minimize to system tray, background tasks continue to run. Click tray icon to reopen window.
settings.minimizeToTrayDisabled: ✗ When closing window, exit app directly, all background tasks will stop.
settings.invalidProxyFormat: Invalid proxy format, please use URL starting with http://, https://, or socks5://
settings.invalidBrowserPath: Invalid browser path, please ensure it's an executable file path (.exe, .app, etc.)
settings.privacyModeDesc: When enabled, email addresses on the interface will be masked
settings.autoSwitchDesc: Automatically switch to another available account when current account balance is below threshold
accounts.title: Account Management
accounts.subtitle: Manage your Kiro IDE accounts
accounts.exportSelectFirst: Please select accounts to export first
accounts.refreshSelectFirst: Please select accounts to refresh first
accounts.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this account?
accounts.confirmDeleteMultiple: Are you sure you want to delete selected {{count}} accounts?
accounts.accountBanned: Account is banned and cannot be used
accounts.tokenInvalid: Token is invalid, please login again
accounts.searchOrCreateTagHint: Enter to search existing tags, or type directly to create new tags
accounts.importFromKiroIDEDescription: Automatically read Kiro IDE cached account information (~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json)
accounts.pleaseLoginToKiroIDEFist: Please log in to Kiro IDE first
accounts.validationInvalidRefreshTokenFo: Row {{index}}: Invalid refreshToken format (should start with aor)
accounts.validationSocialProviderRequire: Row {{index}}: Social accounts must specify provider (Google/Github)
accounts.validationSocialProviderInvalid: Row {{index}}: Social account provider should be Google/Github
accounts.validationIdcProviderInvalid: Row {{index}}: IdC account provider should be BuilderId/Enterprise
accounts.validationEnterpriseRegionRequi: Row {{index}}: Enterprise accounts must provide region field
accounts.validationEnterpriseStartUrlReq: Row {{index}}: Enterprise accounts must provide startUrl field
accounts.networkConnectionFailed:
❌ Network connection failed

Possible reasons:
• Unstable network
• Incorrect proxy settings
• Firewall blocking

Solutions:
1. Check network connection
2. Check proxy settings
3. Disable firewall or add to whitelist
accounts.deleteCurrentAccountMessage:
You are deleting the account currently in use!

After deletion, Kiro IDE will not be usable and you will need to log in again.

Are you sure you want to delete?
accounts.deleteRemoteWarning:
Remote deletion will unregister this account from the AWS service!

This operation cannot be undone and the account will be permanently invalidated.
accounts.batchDeleteIncludesCurrentMessa:
You selected {{count}} accounts, including the account currently in use!

After deletion, Kiro IDE will not be usable and you will need to log in again.

Are you sure you want to delete?

login.agreement: By signing in, you agree to

login.waitingHint: If browser doesn't open automatically, please manually open the authorization page.
login.waitingCloseHint: Clicking cancel will terminate current login process, you can initiate login again later.

about.sponsorNote: Tip: When sponsoring, please note your GitHub username or email for easy contact

hooks.selectToEdit: Select a Hook file to edit

announcement.content2: This project follows CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 open source license, commercial use is prohibited.
announcement.content3: Please download from the official GitHub repository, avoid using untrusted versions.
redeem.desc: Enter purchased card key to automatically add account to list

powers.confirmUninstallPower: Confirm uninstall Power "{{name}}"? Can reinstall from registry after uninstall.
powers.author: Author
powers.name: Name
powers.thirdPartyDisclaimer: Third-party Powers run independently of Kiro core services and may be subject to their own license terms. Kiro has not tested these tools in all scenarios, please only install from trusted sources and review relevant documentation and licenses.
session.title: Session Management
session.subtitle: Manage Kiro IDE's chat sessions
session.selectSessionToView: Select a session to view details
session.deleteWorkspaceConfirm:
Are you sure you want to delete workspace "{{name}}" and all its sessions?

This action cannot be undone!
session.deleteSessionConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete session "{{title}}"?
session.selectWorkspacesFirst: Please select workspaces to delete first
session.cannotFindWorkspace: Cannot find the workspace the session belongs to
gateway.anthropicMessagesApiDesc: Use ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to connect directly to local reverse proxy, supporting native Messages API format.
gateway.openaiApiDesc: Use OPENAI_BASE_URL + OPENAI_API_KEY, supporting both Chat Completions and Responses formats, compatible with standard OpenAI client libraries.
gateway.observabilityPageDesc: Log directory, error history, and request details are all consolidated in the observability page, no need to check system logs.
gateway.fixFormErrors: Please fix form errors before continuing
gateway.gatewayDescription: Consolidate endpoint status, client integration, security boundaries, and observability clues on one screen, prioritizing save/start/restart actions.
gateway.formErrorsBlockSave: Form errors exist, save and start will be blocked.
gateway.formFieldsValid: Current form fields meet reverse proxy startup requirements.
gateway.proxyNotStarted: Reverse proxy not started yet, can start directly with current config.
gateway.pageConfigChanged: Page config has changed, please save config for long-term retention.
gateway.pageConfigSynced: Page config is consistent with config file.
gateway.proxyUsingOldParams: Reverse proxy still using old runtime parameters, needs restart to switch to new config.
gateway.runtimeParamsMatch: Current runtime parameters match page snapshot.
gateway.pageConfigSyncedStatus: Page config is synced with saved state
gateway.opsSuggestionsDesc: First check the top metrics to determine if it's an overall anomaly, then combine with error aggregation to confirm if it's authentication, rate limiting, upstream return, or streaming interruption; finally drill down to recent request details to verify status codes, models, Region, upstream sources, and error information.
gateway.logsDescription: This shows the most recent 120 reverse proxy request records, read from local JSONL files in reverse chronological order. Last sync time:
gateway.searchPlaceholder: Search model, endpoint, IP, error, upstream source or Region
gateway.noMatchingResults: No matching results under current filter conditions, please adjust outcome filter or search keywords.
gateway.noLogsWritten: No reverse proxy requests have been written to local log files yet. Start the reverse proxy and make requests, and recent records will appear here.
gateway.viewDetailedLogs: View detailed logs (error info, request/response body)
gateway.configDescription: Configure core parameters such as listen address, account routing, and security policies
gateway.noApiKeys: No API Keys yet, click 'Generate' or 'Add' button to create
gateway.clientAuthDescription: Clients can use any enabled key for authentication, disabled keys will not be used
gateway.useAllAvailableAccounts: Use all available accounts to maximize resource utilization
gateway.priorityLeastSuccess: Prioritize accounts with the least success count
gateway.roundRobinDescription: Use accounts in rotation in order
gateway.priorityMostQuota: Prioritize accounts with the most remaining quota
gateway.weightedRandomDescription: Weighted random based on health score
gateway.leastConnectionsDescription: Prioritize accounts with the least active connections
gateway.localOnlyAccessDescription: After enabling, only allow 127.0.0.1/::1 access, reject other LAN devices
gateway.ipWhitelistDescription: Allow other LAN devices to access, support single IP or CIDR, separated by newlines or commas
gateway.switchThresholdDescription: Switch when account usage reaches this value
gateway.autoStartDescription: Check to start reverse proxy immediately and auto-start on application startup
gateway.anthropicCompatibilityDesc: Supports Claude-compatible access, message-level streaming returns, account routing, and local authentication.
gateway.openaiCompatibilityDesc: Supports /v1/chat/completions (traditional OpenAI format), /v1/responses, function call, streaming delta, done and completed events, and passes through tool_choice.
gateway.proxyBoundaryDesc: Local entry + upstream credential hosting. Clients only contact local reverse proxy client keys (any configured key works); Kiro access token and region info are automatically managed by the reverse proxy.
gateway.troubleshootingSupportDesc: By default records endpoint, status code, latency, model, Region, upstream source and other metadata; if old logs still contain body, it will also be displayed here for compatibility.
gateway.openaiCompatibilityNote: OpenAI-compatible clients support /v1/responses (Kiro extended format) and /v1/chat/completions (traditional OpenAI format). Example models can be replaced with any model supported by the reverse proxy.
gateway.traditionalOpenAINote: Traditional OpenAI Chat Completions format, compatible with standard OpenAI client libraries (such as openai-python, openai-node).
gateway.claudeCodeNotInstalled: Claude Code CLI not detected, please install first: https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code
gateway.codexNotInstalled: Codex CLI not detected, please install first: https://openai.com/index/introducing-codex-cli/
gateway.claudeCodeToolNote: Claude Code CLI tool, click "Direct Config" to automatically write to ~/.claude/settings.json
gateway.codexToolNote: OpenAI official CLI tool, click "Direct Config" to automatically write to ~/.codex/config.toml and ~/.codex/auth.json
gateway.clientToProxy: Client -> Local Reverse Proxy uses API Key
gateway.proxyToKiro: Local Reverse Proxy -> Kiro API uses local access token

hooks.promptPlaceholder: Please fill in execution instructions here

welcome.supportMessage: If this tool helps you, feel free to buy me a coffee. Your support is my motivation to keep updating ❤️
welcome.sponsorHint: Tip: Please include your GitHub username or email when sponsoring for easy contact

errors.remoteDeleteNotSupported: Remote deletion is not supported for this account type
errors.noCliAccounts: No account data found in database
errors.multipleCliAccounts: Multiple accounts found in database, please contact the developer

errors.clientApiKeyInvalid: Client API Key is not configured or invalid
errors.noAvailableAccount: No available account found matching the gateway configuration
errors.loadBalancerFailed: Load balancer failed to select an available account
